2603.28735.title: RAD-AI: Rethinking Architecture Documentation for AI-Augmented Ecosystems
2603.28735.paper_summary: AI-augmented ecosystems (interconnected systems where multiple AI components interact through shared data and infrastructure) are becoming the architectural norm for smart cities, autonomous fleets, and intelligent platforms. Yet the architecture documentation frameworks practitioners rely on, arc42 and the C4 model, were designed for deterministic software and cannot capture probabilistic behavior, data-dependent evolution, or dual ML/software lifecycles. This gap carries regulatory consequence: the EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689) mandates technical documentation through Annex IV that no existing framework provides structured support for, with enforcement for high-risk systems beginning August 2, 2026. We present RAD-AI, a backward-compatible extension framework that augments arc42 with eight AI-specific sections and C4 with three diagram extensions, complemented by a systematic EU AI Act Annex IV compliance mapping. A regulatory coverage assessment with six experienced software-architecture practitioners provides preliminary evidence that RAD-AI increases Annex IV addressability from approximately 36% to 93% (mean rating) and demonstrates substantial improvement over existing frameworks. Comparative analysis on two production AI platforms (Uber Michelangelo, Netflix Metaflow) captures eight additional AI-specific concerns missed by standard frameworks and demonstrates that documentation deficiencies are structural rather than domain-specific. An illustrative smart mobility ecosystem case study reveals ecosystem-level concerns, including cascading drift and differentiated compliance obligations, that are invisible under standard notation.
2603.28735.paper_summary_zh: AI增強生態系統（多個AI組件通過共享數據和基礎設施互動的互聯系統）正成為智慧城市、自主車隊和智能平台的建築規範。<lb/>然而，從業者所依賴的架構文檔框架，arc42和C4模型，是為確定性軟件設計的，無法捕捉概率行為、數據依賴的演變或雙重機器學習/軟件生命週期。<lb/>這一差距帶來了監管後果：歐盟AI法案（法規2024/1689）要求通過附錄IV提供技術文檔，而現有框架並未提供結構化支持，對高風險系統的執行將於2026年8月2日開始。<lb/>我們提出RAD-AI，一個向後兼容的擴展框架，通過八個AI特定部分增強arc42，並通過三個圖表擴展增強C4，並輔以系統化的EU AI法案附錄IV合規映射。<lb/>與六位經驗豐富的軟件架構從業者進行的監管覆蓋評估提供了初步證據，顯示RAD-AI將附錄IV的可達性從約36%提高到93%（平均評分），並顯示出相較於現有框架的顯著改進。<lb/>對兩個生產AI平台（Uber Michelangelo，Netflix Metaflow）的比較分析捕捉到標準框架未能關注的八個額外AI特定問題，並證明文檔缺陷是結構性的，而非領域特定的。<lb/>一個示例性的智慧移動生態系統案例研究揭示了生態系統層面的問題，包括級聯漂移和差異化合規義務，這些在標準符號下是不可見的。
2603.28735.author: Oliver Aleksander Larsen et.al.
2603.28735.authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
